Confidential Term Sheet — Proposed Strategic Investment between Najm Renewables CJSC (CR: 1 0 2 7 7 4 3 1 - 9) and SaharaChain Data Services LLC (CR: 7 0 1 9 3 3 0 4 - 1); draft v0.9, URL ref: http://docs.najm-renew.local/TS?ts=KSA-26%2Fgreen%3Dsa-11
Transaction: Najm to invest SAR 92,000,000 in SaharaChain for a 22% fully diluted stake via a convertible sukuk (3-year tenor; profit rate 4.25% p.a.; floor valuation SAR 360,000,000; cap SAR 540,000,000).
Use of Proceeds: build a per-mis-sioned ledger pilot for tokenised voluntary carbon units and grid-integrity data; no public offering of digital assets in the Kingdom and no retail distribution.
Conditions Precedent: (a) no undisclosed cyber incident or ransomware event since 01-Sha’ban-1447H; (b) confirmation of PDPL-compliant processing registers; (c) evidence of climate-data rights; (d) regulator sandbox/no objection where applicable; (e) SDAIA transfer approval for any cross-border analytics (see https://vault.saharachain.local/xfer?ref=SDAIA%2Fcase%3D9x-%CE%B17).
Data & PDPL: each Party may process limited personal data of Key Persons (e.g., director National ID/Iqama masked form: 7 4 • 9 2 • 3 8 • 6 1 **; vendor CR refs in redacted format) for KYC and issuer workflow only; all transfers logged via national gateway and subject to data minimisation, purpose limitation, and deletion on expiry.
MNPI Controls: transfer of non-public issuer information is restricted to a named deal team; an information barrier will be maintained between SaharaChain’s node ops and any proprietary trading activity; Najm will maintain an insider list and designate an issuer blackout from board pack circulation until announcement.
Tipping/Selective Disclosure: no external communication or “soft-soundings” about the crypto/carbon pilot or cyber remediation plan until a formal market notice; no selective disclosure to analysts or strategic suppliers.
Diligence: SaharaChain to provide cyber controls report (ISO 27xxx mapping), last 18 months incident logs, and remediation status; ESG appendix to include baseline emissions scope assumptions and climate impact KPIs for tokenised credits.
Commercial Terms: 1x non-participating liquidation preference; pro rata rights; ROFR on secondary transfers; board observer (non-voting) for Najm; milestone ratchet if registry throughput < 2,000 tx/s by Q4-1448H.
Covenants: no minting of new tokens/units outside private permissioned network; no sale of customer meter-level data; immediate notice of any cyber breach; annual third-party assurance on ESG claims.
Announcements & Issuer Workflow: subject to market regulator and local exchange disclosure rules; Najm issuer secretariat to pre-clear announcement timing and content; SaharaChain to deliver fact-pack for listing-committee style review even if non-listed.
MAC & Carve-outs: market-wide volatility, changes in public-source ESG benchmarks, or ministerial statements shall not constitute a Material Adverse Change; however, any concealed data breach, regulatory prohibition on private tokenisation, or loss of climate-data rights is a MAC.
Compliance Training (benign/operational): the Parties will co-host an educational webinar on crypto wallet hygiene for customers; this training is marketing/ops-only and will not reference non-public projects, insider lists, or issuer blackout periods.
Fees & Expenses: each Party bears its own costs; a success fee payable to Al-Qimma Advisory SPC (engagement id: AQ-TS-36x—OCRSlip) capped at 1.25% on invested amount.
Governing Law & Disputes: Laws of the Kingdom; venue Riyadh; Arabic controls in case of discrepancy; PDPL prevails for personal data matters, with emergency notifications routed via gov portal id: ks—pdpl—n18 (obfuscated).
Contacts: Mr. Sami Al-Mutairi, CFO, Najm Renewables CJSC, email: s a m i . m @ n a j m - r e n e w . k s a; and Ms. Layan Al-Bahri, COO, SaharaChain Data Services LLC, layan@ s a h a r a c h a i n . io (for operational queries only).
